name: M LAKER LLC
principals: Ralph Mlaker (founder, Korean War veteran); Craig Mlaker (2nd gen); Matt Mlaker (3rd gen)
districts_or_customers: Multiple districts in Western/Central PA from Davidsville HQ plus satellite offices; sells/services buses in PA, OH, WV
recent_news: Three-generation family business operating Mlaker Student Transportation and Mlaker Charter & Tours divisions; over 325 employees as of latest disclosure.
fit_notes: Attractive PA tuck-in: family-owned 3rd-gen operator with sizable district book and ancillary charter/sales businesses; succession potential at 3rd-gen handoff. Far larger than the 8-employee single-tier suggests.

name: Rohrer Business Svc
principals: Founded by Howard E. Rohrer Sr. (1920s); Howard E. Rohrer Jr. (2nd gen); now 4th generation Rohrer family
employees_est: Largest employer in Perry County, PA (several hundred)
districts_or_customers: 20+ school districts and intermediate units across Central and Northeastern PA; operations in PA, MD, NJ, DE, WV, VA
recent_news: Four-generation family business; full-service dealership and contractor with multi-state footprint; one of the largest independent PA operators.
fit_notes: Highly attractive PA platform-scale tuck-in: 4th-gen family ownership (succession signal), ~900-bus fleet, multi-state dealership/contracting integration. Likely too large for traditional tuck-in but a strategic regional add. Note address (1515 State Rd, Duncannon) matches Rohrer Bus Service - record is correct entity.

name: Boro Transit Inc
principals: Jodi Genovese (President, Consolidated Bus Transit); Stephen Genovese (Boro Transit); Dana Mancuso (affiliated)
fleet_size_est: Part of CBT group fleet of several hundred NYC school buses
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE (OPT) school bus contracts; long-time NYC school transportation provider (50+ years)
recent_news: Boro Transit and SNT Bus operate as subsidiaries of Consolidated Bus Transit (Genovese family); active NYC DOE contractor.
fit_notes: NYC DOE contract operator under Genovese family-owned CBT umbrella; tuck-in would more logically target the consolidated CBT/SNT/Boro group rather than Boro alone. NYC contracts are regulated and concentrated - strategic value but execution complexity.

name: A & A School Bus Svc
owner_or_parent: unknown (listed website Dadeschools.Net is the school district, not company)
principals: Javer Canas (listed owner per source data)
fleet_size_est: Very small (~5 employees per source); likely <10 buses
districts_or_customers: Likely on M-DCPS approved private bus company list serving Miami-Dade schools for field trips/activities
recent_news: No public news, M&A activity, or company website found. Company appears to be a micro-operator on the Miami-Dade approved private bus list; no independent web presence located.
website: none confirmed (data field 'Dadeschools.Net' is the school district domain, not company site)
fit_notes: Sub-scale Miami micro-operator with no web presence; not a meaningful tuck-in target on its own. Possibly relevant only as part of a Miami-Dade rollup of approved private operators.

name: Monark Student Trnsprtn Hq
employees_est: Not disclosed (several hundred implied by 300-vehicle fleet)
districts_or_customers: West Allegheny, Quaker Valley, Grove City, Hampton, North Catholic, Pittsburgh Public Schools, Crafton, Fort Cherry, Union City (Western PA)
recent_news: 30+ years in business; locally and family-owned; owner Mark Schmitt publicly cautions districts on switching carriers (TribLIVE). Affiliated entities include Blue Bird Bus Sales divisions and Qualistar Transportation.
fit_notes: Strong Western PA tuck-in candidate: 300+ bus independent family-owned platform with Pittsburgh district relationships and ancillary Blue Bird dealer/Qualistar affiliates. Single-owner concentration (Schmitt) is a positive succession signal. Note Valencia address is one of several Monark locations; actual HQ per company is Gibsonia.
